opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I just noticed these matching white, dead-looking spots on both the backs of my hands. I think I've caught a terrible fungal infection or parasite. Please, is this something eating my skin?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Noticed the white patches tonight while washing my hands and completely panicked — I need to know if it's contagious and spreading.

symptom_narrative.onset: I only really noticed them tonight while I was washing my hands, but thinking about it they might have been slowly coming on for a while without me paying attention
symptom_narrative.duration: noticed acutely tonight; possibly present quietly for weeks or months before that
symptom_narrative.character: small chalk-white patches, like the colour has completely gone — they look dead and matching on both hands
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the spots themselves don't hurt, but I'm terrified — I had a full panic attack when I saw them and couldn't stop shaking
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the backs of both hands, in pretty much the same spots on each side
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: they're just there all the time; the redness came on tonight after I scrubbed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — I keep washing them hoping it'll clear
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I've been scrubbing them over and over with really hot water and strong soap and hand sanitiser, and now they're sore and red
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain in the white spots, no itching, no flaking or peeling; I don't feel sick otherwise
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before that I've noticed
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't stop staring at my hands and washing them — it's all I can think about tonight

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Very distressed tonight; has been repeatedly washing hands with hot water, harsh soap and multiple sanitisers out of fear of contamination.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Rapid, frightened messages fixated on contagion and 'something eating my skin'; seeks urgent certainty and reassurance.

objective_data_offered.value: small bright chalk-white patches on the backs of both hands, matching in roughly the same place on each side
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen over a phone camera in home lighting; borders and any fine surface change cannot be assessed remotely

objective_data_offered.value: the skin around the white patches looks pink/red and sore
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: redness is recent and follows vigorous washing; a remote view cannot grade it

objective_data_offered.value: patient appears very anxious and is breathing fast, repeatedly inspecting and rubbing the hands on camera
sink area cluttered with multiple harsh hand sanitisers and soaps
